title: Offline Information Systems and Mesh Services

documents.title: Local Service Priority Stack
documents.content: When internet is intermittent, prioritize local services that preserve function: messaging relay, incident board, map mirror, medical protocols, inventory dashboard, and document library. Design for text-first interfaces that remain usable on low bandwidth and old hardware.

documents.title: Mesh Topology Planning
documents.content: Use a hybrid mesh with neighborhood nodes and a few backbone links between high points. Avoid single points of failure by ensuring every critical site has at least two independent paths. Validate topology with periodic link-failure drills.

documents.title: Sneakernet and Data Courier Workflow
documents.content: For disconnected zones, move updates via encrypted removable media with signed manifests and checksum verification. Keep write-once update bundles and rollback copies. Physical data courier routes should be scheduled and logged just like supply convoys.

documents.title: Offline Maps and Gazetteers
documents.content: Store regional maps locally in multiple formats (raster, vector, printed). Maintain a gazetteer index listing water points, clinics, shelters, bridges, fuel, and hazards. Update map notes after field patrols and version them so outdated copies are easy to identify.

documents.title: Identity and Access in Low-Infra Environments
documents.content: Use role-based accounts for local services and avoid shared admin credentials. Keep emergency break-glass credentials sealed and audited. Rotate credentials after personnel changes, and maintain paper backup procedures in case systems become unavailable.

documents.title: 3-2-1 Backups Without Cloud
documents.content: Keep three copies of critical data, on two different media types, with one physically separated copy. Test restore procedures monthly. Backups are not real until restored successfully under realistic constraints.

documents.title: File Integrity and Update Safety
documents.content: Use checksums and signed release notes for updates to prevent corruption and tampering. Deploy updates in stages: pilot node first, then wider rollout. Keep prior known-good versions to enable fast rollback if failures appear.

documents.title: Information Governance and Trust
documents.content: Define who can publish alerts, medical advisories, and movement notices. Label all critical posts with source, timestamp, and expiration window. Misinformation spreads fastest during uncertainty; clear governance and versioned updates reduce panic.
